---
title: ""
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: extarticle
    fontsize: 20pt
    classoption: [landscape]
    geometry:
      - paperwidth=48in
      - paperheight=36in
      - margin=0.55in
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{multicol}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{titlesec}
          \usepackage{enumitem}
          \usepackage{parskip}
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{booktabs}
          \usepackage{array}
          \usepackage{tikz}
          \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta,positioning,calc}
          \usepackage{qrcode}
          \usepackage[scaled=0.92]{helvet}
          \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}

          \definecolor{ggblue}{HTML}{0B3061}
          \definecolor{ggdeep}{HTML}{05203F}
          \definecolor{gggold}{HTML}{8C6F2C}
          \definecolor{soft}{HTML}{F1F5F9}
          \definecolor{accent}{HTML}{1E3A8A}
          \definecolor{ok}{HTML}{166534}
          \definecolor{rowalt}{HTML}{F3F6FB}

          \pagestyle{empty}
          \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
          \setlength{\parskip}{0.45em}
          \setlength{\columnsep}{0.42in}
          \setlength{\columnseprule}{0.4pt}
          \setlist[itemize]{leftmargin=1.3em,topsep=2pt,itemsep=3pt,parsep=0pt}
          \setlist[enumerate]{leftmargin=1.5em,topsep=2pt,itemsep=3pt,parsep=0pt}

          \titleformat{\section}
            {\Huge\bfseries\color{ggblue}}
            {}{0em}{}
            [\vspace{-0.35em}{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{1.2pt}}]
          \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{0.6em}{0.4em}
          \titleformat{\subsection}
            {\LARGE\bfseries\color{accent}}
            {}{0em}{}
          \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{0.6em}{0.2em}
---

\noindent
\begin{minipage}[c]{0.78\linewidth}
\vspace*{-0.2in}
{\fontsize{56}{64}\selectfont\bfseries\color{ggdeep} Grounded Study Item Generation\\from Personal Notes}

\vspace{0.12in}

{\fontsize{30}{36}\selectfont\color{accent} An Obsidian Plugin with a Multi-Agent Pipeline \& Per-Concept Evaluation}

\vspace{0.14in}

{\fontsize{22}{26}\selectfont\textbf{Chaowei Wang} \quad \textbf{Yubo Xing} \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad Georgetown University, DSAN 6725 --- Applied GenAI for AI Developers, Spring 2026}
\end{minipage}\hfill
\begin{minipage}[c]{0.20\linewidth}
\centering
\fcolorbox{ggblue}{white}{\begin{minipage}{0.94\linewidth}\centering
\vspace{4pt}
\qrcode[height=2.4in]{https://github.com/gu-dsan6725/spring-2026-final-project-team_24}\\[4pt]
{\large\bfseries\color{ggblue}Code, paper, slides}\\
{\small\texttt{gu-dsan6725 / team\_24}}
\vspace{4pt}
\end{minipage}}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.05in}
{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{2.4pt}}
\vspace{0.08in}

\begin{multicols}{4}

\section*{Problem}

Personal note vaults (Obsidian, Notion, Logseq) accumulate \textbf{concept notes, PDFs, and figures}, but turning them into study items --- problems, flashcards, code challenges --- is manual, time-consuming, and error-prone.

LLMs draft items cheaply but routinely \textbf{drift} from the exact content the learner stored, breaking instructional trust. Grading drift is worse: a single scalar score punishes \emph{every concept} on a failed answer, including ones the student never even tried to use.

\subsection*{Goal}

Generate study items grounded in the \textbf{learner's own notes}, grade them at the \textbf{concept level}, and never move raw notes outside the vault.

\section*{Approach}

The vault is the source of truth. A TypeScript plugin sends curated JSON views of selected concepts to a FastAPI service that runs a multi-agent loop under tool-calling (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5). All outputs are written back to the same vault as markdown.

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Sample Evaluator} pre-grades a learner-supplied reference --- markdown \emph{or} an image of an exam clip --- and threads pedagogical \texttt{analysis\_notes} into the Generator.
  \item \textbf{Core hardening loop} (Generator $\to$ Actor $\to$ Reflector) refines items until they survive simulated solving; a back-edge re-emits when difficulty $\geq$ hard.
  \item \textbf{Answer Evaluator} returns per-concept verdicts: \texttt{correct}, \texttt{alt\_path}, \texttt{misunderstood}, \texttt{untested}.
  \item \textbf{Mastery + depth-aware scheduler}: per-concept Elo updates; mastered concepts graduate to integration items; \texttt{focus\_depth} advances along a topological ordering produced by a link-architect over MinerU-extracted PDF sections.
\end{itemize}

\subsection*{Why per-concept verdicts}

Most LLM evaluators emit a scalar. We emit a \textbf{list of \texttt{ConceptVerdict} objects} so an alternate solution path still credits the concept it exercised, and a wrong answer never penalizes concepts the learner did not touch --- directly feeding the Elo update and the difficulty progression.

\columnbreak

\section*{System Architecture}

\begin{center}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{%
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  font=\footnotesize,
  >=Latex,
  arr/.style={->, thick},
  lbl/.style={inner sep=1pt, fill=white, pos=0.45},
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, inner sep=5pt, minimum width=2.6cm},
  small/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, inner sep=3pt, minimum width=2.05cm, minimum height=7mm},
  agent/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, inner sep=2pt, minimum height=6mm, minimum width=1.45cm}
]
  \node[box, minimum width=3.2cm] (vault) {Obsidian vault\\(notes, PDFs)};
  \node[box, below=8mm of vault] (plugin) {Obsidian plugin\\(TypeScript)};
  \node[box, right=24mm of plugin] (api) {FastAPI\\backend};
  \draw[arr] (vault) -- (plugin);
  \draw[arr] (plugin) -- node[lbl, above] {\texttt{HTTP}} (api);

  \node[small, anchor=north] (ex) at ($(api.south)+(-3.4,-4mm-0.85)$) {Extraction /\\paper ingest};
  \node[small, anchor=north] (vec) at ($(api.south)+(0,-4mm-0.85)$) {Vectors\\(index / search)};
  \node[small, anchor=north] (sess) at ($(api.south)+(3.4,-4mm-0.85)$) {Item session};
  \draw[arr, shorten >=2.5pt] (api.south) -| (ex.north);
  \draw[arr, shorten >=2.5pt] (api.south) -- (vec.north);
  \draw[arr, shorten >=2.5pt] (api.south) -| (sess.north);

  \node[small, below=7mm of ex] (mineru) {MinerU};
  \node[small, below=7mm of vec] (pine) {Pinecone};
  \draw[arr] (ex) -- node[right, xshift=1mm] {\tiny extract} (mineru);
  \draw[arr] (vec) -- node[right, xshift=1mm] {\tiny index} (pine);

  \node[agent, below=14mm of sess] (F) {Feasibility};
  \node[agent, right=2mm of F] (G) {Generator};
  \node[agent, right=2mm of G] (A) {Actors};
  \node[agent, right=2mm of A] (R) {Reflector};
  \node[agent, right=2mm of R] (Gr) {Round\\evaluator};
  \foreach \a/\b in {F/G, G/A, A/R, R/Gr}
    \draw[arr] (\a.east) -- (\b.west);
  \draw[arr, dashed, shorten <=1pt, shorten >=1pt]
    (R.south) .. controls +(0,-0.55) and +(0,-0.55) .. (G.south)
    node[pos=0.5, below, font=\tiny, inner sep=1pt] {refine / harden};
  \node[above=1pt of R, font=\tiny] {$\pm$ hardening};
  \draw[arr, shorten >=2pt] (sess.south) -- (F.north);

  \node[agent, above=5mm of G, minimum width=1.7cm] (SA) {Sample\\evaluator};
  \draw[arr, shorten >=1pt]
    (sess.south east) .. controls +(0.6,-0.4) and +(0,0.6) .. (SA.north);
  \draw[arr, shorten >=1pt]
    (SA.south) -- node[lbl, right, font=\tiny] {analysis\_notes} (G.north);

  \node[box, minimum width=2.2cm, below=12mm of G] (ag) {Answer evaluator\\(per-concept)};
  \node[box, minimum width=2.0cm, left=16mm of ag] (user) {Learner\\(replies)};
  \draw[arr, shorten <=1pt, shorten >=1pt]
    ($(user.east)+(0,2.2mm)$) -- node[midway, above, font=\tiny, inner sep=1pt] {submit}
    ($(ag.west)+(0,2.2mm)$);
  \draw[arr, shorten <=1pt, shorten >=1pt]
    ($(ag.west)+(0,-2.2mm)$) -- node[midway, below, font=\tiny, inner sep=1pt] {verdicts}
    ($(user.east)+(0,-2.2mm)$);

  \draw[arr, shorten <=1pt, shorten >=1pt]
    (ag.east) .. controls +(1.0,0) and +(0.4,-1.6) .. (sess.south)
    node[pos=0.55, right, font=\tiny, inner sep=1pt] {mastery + focus\_depth};
\end{tikzpicture}}\\[3pt]
{\small\itshape Vault $\to$ plugin $\to$ FastAPI; multimodal \emph{Sample Evaluator} pre-grades references into \texttt{analysis\_notes}; the staged generation loop hardens items; per-concept \emph{Answer Evaluator} verdicts feed mastery + depth-aware scheduling back into the next round.}
\end{center}

\subsection*{Agent roles}

\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
\begin{tabularx}{\linewidth}{@{}>{\bfseries\color{ggblue}}l X@{}}
\toprule
Feasibility & Routes a request to \texttt{abandon} / \texttt{review} / \texttt{generate}. \\
Generator & Tool-calling JSON; outputs validated against declared concepts. \\
Actors$\times$3 & Simulate solving to surface failure modes. \\
Reflector & Quality score + bounded refinement; hardening directives. \\
Round eval & Per-round summary; supports difficulty progression. \\
Sample eval & Pre-grades md/image reference $\to$ \texttt{analysis\_notes}. \\
Answer eval & Per-concept verdicts $\to$ Elo mastery. \\
Link-architect & Topological depth over PDF sections (ingest). \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabularx}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.95\linewidth,height=3.6in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-foundation.png}\\[1pt]
{\small\itshape One paper PDF $\to$ 21 section concepts, deduplicated.}
\end{center}

\columnbreak

\section*{Plugin in Action}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/screen-graph-view.png}\\[2pt]
{\small\itshape Obsidian global graph view --- each concept becomes a node; the Item Generator panel reads selected sources directly from the vault.}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.12in}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/screen-item-rendered.png}\\[2pt]
{\small\itshape A generated \emph{very\_hard} problem combining Bayes' theorem with sequential testing under time-degrading sensitivity. \LaTeX{} renders inline; the Session panel tracks rounds and exposes ``Grade My Answers'' / ``Next Round'' controls.}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.1in}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.45\linewidth,height=8in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-coverage.png}\hspace{0.2in}\includegraphics[width=0.45\linewidth,height=8in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-options.png}\\[2pt]
{\small\itshape Left: concept coverage row --- each cell is one concept colored by highest difficulty exercised; tick marks delineate dependency depths and the bolder tick marks \texttt{focus\_depth}. \quad Right: generation options panel.}
\end{center}

\columnbreak

\section*{Pilot Evaluation \,(N=2)}

Offline harness over the shipped \texttt{dataset.json} via Braintrust. \textbf{No baselines}; our system only.

\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.12}
\begin{tabularx}{\linewidth}{@{}X r r r@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Scorer} & \textbf{Mean} & \textbf{Min} & \textbf{Max} \\
\midrule
NoRunError                       & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
FeasibilityAlign                 & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
OutputNonEmpty                   & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
FoundationCoverage               & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
ProblemPromptShape               & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
AnswerLength                     & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
BodyLengthBand                   & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
ProxyTokenEfficiency             & \color{ok}\textbf{100\%} & 1.00 & 1.00 \\
RubricSatisfaction (LLM judge)   & \color{ok}\textbf{91\%}  & 0.82 & 1.00 \\
Latency (band score)             & \color{ok}\textbf{87.5\%}& 0.75 & 1.00 \\
ItemStructural                   & \color{ok}\textbf{85\%}  & 0.70 & 1.00 \\
ItemClarity (LLM judge)          & 69\%                     & 0.38 & 1.00 \\
MarkdownStructure                & 46\%                     & 0.35 & 0.57 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabularx}

\vspace{2pt}
{\small\itshape With $N{=}2$ these numbers \textbf{characterize the harness}, not a benchmark; CIs are wide and unreported, means are descriptive only.}

\section*{Post-pilot additions}

Three features landed \emph{after} the eval was frozen --- covered by unit tests but not yet in the table:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Per-concept \textbf{Answer Evaluator} + Elo mastery loop.
  \item Multimodal \textbf{Sample Evaluator} (markdown or exam-clip image).
  \item Depth-aware \textbf{scheduler} + concept coverage visualization.
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Responsible AI}

Raw notes stay in the vault. Cloud LLM and embedding calls are opt-in. The multimodal sample evaluator base64-encodes images in the browser before forwarding --- a new exfiltration surface that is surfaced in-session only and deduplicated across evaluator and generator calls. Per-concept verdicts also reduce a known grading-drift failure mode where a single scalar score punishes concepts the learner never even attempted.

\section*{Limitations \& Future Work}

\begin{itemize}
  \item In-memory sessions; not durable across restarts.
  \item Groq path is text-only; sample images stripped with a warning.
  \item MinerU PDF ingest brittle on multi-column layouts and low-quality scans.
  \item \textbf{Future:} stratified eval, human ratings, durable mastery storage, Groq multimodal, larger labeled trajectory set for the Answer Evaluator.
\end{itemize}

\end{multicols}

\vspace{0.05in}
{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{1pt}}
\vspace{0.05in}
\begin{center}
{\large\itshape Code, paper, slides, and tests:\;\texttt{github.com/gu-dsan6725/spring-2026-final-project-team\_24} \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad DSAN 6725 --- Applied GenAI for AI Developers --- Spring 2026 \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad Scan QR (top right) for repo, paper PDF, slide deck, and demo video}
\end{center}
